output.title: Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit? Here's How to Get It Back Fast
output.meta_description: Is your landlord withholding your security deposit? Learn your tenant rights, what landlords can deduct, and how to demand your deposit back in writing.
content_outline: Introduction: The Real Cost of a Landlord Withholding Your Security Deposit
Know Your Timeline: How Long a Landlord Legally Has to Return Your Deposit
Legal Deductions vs. Wrongful Charges: What a Landlord Can and Cannot Deduct
Step-by-Step: What to Do When Your Landlord Won't Return Your Deposit
How to Write an Effective Security Deposit Dispute Letter
Escalation Options: Small Claims Court and State Penalties
Conclusion: Reclaim Your Money Without the Stress
key_points: Most states require landlords to return security deposits within 14 to 60 days after move-out.
Landlords cannot deduct for normal wear and tear; only actual damages and unpaid rent are typically allowed.
A formal demand letter is the fastest, most effective first step to recover a withheld deposit before filing suit.
DepositBack automates state-compliant demand letters to increase recovery speed and reduce tenant stress.
output.cta: Stop waiting and start recovering. Create your free, state-specific security deposit demand letter with DepositBack in under 5 minutes—no lawyer needed.
output.target_audience: Frustrated U.S. renters whose landlords are wrongfully withholding, delaying, or refusing to return a security deposit.
